source_subject: Vital Knowledge: Vital Dawn for Friday April 10, 2026
market_levels:
Market levels – the US equity futures are seeing losses so far this morning: S&P -8.75 points/13bp, Dow -96 points/20bp, Nasdaq -33 points/13bp, and R2 K -7.3 points/28bp. The major Eurozone indices area flat-to-up 10bp, with outperformance in luxury, tech, media, and healthcare while utilities, energy, telecoms, insurance, and basic resources underperform . Stocks in Asia generally saw gains on Friday: TPX -0.04%, NKY +1.84%, Hang Seng +0.55%, HSCEI +0.5%, SHCOMP +0.51%, Shenzhen +1.54%, Taiwan TAIEX +1.6%, Korea KOSPI +1.4%, Australia ASX 200 -0.14%, and India is up ~90bp. Treasuries are for sale, w/yields up ~2-3bp across the curve ahead of the Mar CPI that’s expected to be very hot . The market is pricing in ~6bp of Fed rate cuts this year. The DXY is ticking up 13bp following several days of declines. Brent is rising ~220bp to ~$98 while gold falls 50bp and silver drops 30bp. Bitcoin is sliding 100bp to ~$71.66 K.
•
whats_happening:
What’s happening this morning – it’s fairly quiet as investors remain on the edge of their seats regarding the Middle East ceasefire ahead of the upcoming talks between US and Iranian officials in Pakistan (Iranian officials are on the ground in Pakistan according to reports, and the meeting is set to occur early Sat morning Pakistan time). The two big impediments to a deal include Hormuz (traffic remains very depressed, and Tehran seems to be pushing forward with plans for a toll, something Trump Thurs night warned against) and Lebanon (Netanyahu agreed to dial back the intensity of IDF strikes and will hold talks with the Lebanon gov’t, but Israel vowed to continue its campaign against Hezbollah). In terms of non-Iran news , we didn’t see anything too dramatic since the Thurs close ( China’s inflation was mixed, the Bank of Korea left rates unchanged as expected, TSMC’s Q1 revenue came in a bit better w/Mar sales surging ~45% Y/Y vs. ~22% in Feb, The Information published Space X P&L details from 2025 that seem to fall far short of the types of numbers required for a ~$1.75 T+ market cap, and the latest Barron’s cover story talks about a Space X/Tesla merger ).
•
thinking_about_markets:
How we’re thinking about markets – there’s still a lot of cynicism and skepticism about the durability of the ceasefire and to the extent the détente’s permanency outperforms expectations (which is our view), this will place further upward pressure on the SPX , especially given the technical backdrop (characterized by lean long positioning and negative sentiment). However, the SPX has already bounced ~8% trough-to-peak in just a few days, and the index is nearing the ~6900-7000 zone , which was a problem area even before the war started . And while we have a brighter view on the Middle East ceasefire than the consensus , the region will never go back to where it stood before (~$90 is probably the new ~$70 when it comes to Brent), and a substantial burst of stagflationary pressure is washing over the global economy from the last 5 weeks of fighting (the US CPI for Mar due out Fri morning is expected to show a significant acceleration vs. Feb).
• What we’ll be watching on Friday – the major events will be the US CPI for Mar at 8:30am ET (the Street is looking for a sharp acceleration in headline to +3.4% vs. +2.4% in Feb while core climbs to +2.7% vs. +2.5% in Feb) and the first meeting of US and Iranian officials in Pakistan (which is set to commence Sat morning Pakistan time). In addition, a hearing will take place before the Court of Int’l Trade about Trump’s 10% Section 122 blanket tariff that was implemented after the SCOTUS decision (and that Trump said would go to 15% but hasn’t).
•
market_in_a_minute_macro:
Market in a Minute (macro) – US is considering options to encourage ships to begin traveling through Hormuz in greater numbers ( Axios ); Trump is demanding commitments from NATO allies to help provide support for securing Hormuz ( Reuters ); Trump told NBC News that he was “very optimistic” about reaching a deal w/Iran (“Iran’s leaders talk much differently when you’re at a meeting than they do to the press . They’re much more reasonable . They’re agreeing to all the things that they have to agree to”) ( NBC ); China/US relations : China is playing a key role in helping negotiate peace in the Middle East and Xi hopes this will set the stage for a successful summit with Trump next month ( WSJ ); China/US relations: the White House is deliberately adopting a more conciliatory approach to China compared to the antagonism of Trump’s first term ( WSJ ); China’s PPI came in a bit firmer than anticipated at +0.5% Y/Y (vs. the Street +0.4% and up from -0.9% in Feb) while the CPI ran a touch cooler at +1% (vs. the Street +1.1% and down from +1.3% in Feb) ( Reuters ); Warsh’s confirmation hearing, preliminarily planned for Thurs 4/16, will be pushed back ( Punchbowl , CNBC )
•

• What bulls are talking about : while there were ceasefire violations earlier Wed, on Thurs and into Fri the détente between the US/Israel and Iran was holding in place reasonably well . Trump told NBC News that he was “very optimistic” about reaching a deal w/Iran (“Iran’s leaders talk much differently when you’re at a meeting than they do to the press. They’re much more reasonable. They’re agreeing to all the things that they have to agree to”). Importantly, it looks like the situation in Lebanon could cool , removing a substantial source of pressure from the Iran ceasefire . Netanyahu on Thurs said Israel was seeking to “ open direct negotiations with Lebanon as soon as possible ” (according to an NBC News report, Trump asked Netanyahu to dial back the intensity of the IDF’s strikes on Lebanon, and the Israeli PM agreed “to be a helpful partner” ).
• What bears are talking about : the Middle East ceasefire is very fragile and risks being undermined by the situation in Lebanon ( Netanyahu might be seeking to hold talks w/Lebanon, but he was explicit in stating that no cease fire exists between the two countries , and vowed to continue striking Hezbollah “with force” ) and Hormuz ( volumes through the waterway remain depressed , and Tehran seems very serious about proceeding with plans for a toll, something Trump at 5:10pm ET Thurs afternoon warned them not to do). The US and Iran have some huge differences that need to be ironed out for a full deal to be implemented, and time is very much of the essence (Trump warned Wed night that the Pentagon will resume its campaign against Iran if an agreement isn’t reached ). Even if the US and Iran are able to secure a permanent détente, the situation in the Middle East won’t go back to the way it was in the near or immediate term – every commodity and product going through Hormuz will embed a significant geopolitical risk premium for the foreseeable future , ensuring the global economy faces a persistent source of stagflationary pressure . Significant damage was inflicted to key infrastructure in the Middle East over the last several weeks, and it will take a long time to bring all this back online ( Saudi Arabia on Thurs said it lost ~600 K BPD of oil capacity due to damage from the war). The IMF on Thurs warned that the Middle East war will deal a major blow to the global economy , even with the recent ceasefire.

energy, telecoms, insurance, and basic resources underperform . Stocks in Asia generally saw gains on Friday: TPX -0.04%, NKY +1.84%, Hang Seng +0.55%, HSCEI +0.5%, SHCOMP +0.51%, Shenzhen +1.54%, Taiwan TAIEX +1.6%, Korea KOSPI +1.4%, Australia ASX 200 -0.14%, and India is up ~90bp. Treasuries are for sale, w/yields up ~2-3bp across the curve ahead of the Mar CPI that’s expected to be very hot . The market is pricing in ~6bp of Fed rate cuts this year. The DXY is ticking up 13bp following several days of declines. Brent is rising ~220bp to ~$98 while gold falls 50bp and silver drops 30bp. Bitcoin is sliding 100bp to ~$71.66 K.
• What’s happening this morning – it’s fairly quiet as investors remain on the edge of their seats regarding the Middle East ceasefire ahead of the upcoming talks between US and Iranian officials in Pakistan (Iranian officials are on the ground in Pakistan according to reports, and the meeting is set to occur early Sat morning Pakistan time). The two big impediments to a deal include Hormuz (traffic remains very depressed, and Tehran seems to be pushing forward with plans for a toll, something Trump Thurs night warned against) and Lebanon (Netanyahu agreed to dial back the intensity of IDF strikes and will hold talks with the Lebanon gov’t, but Israel vowed to continue its campaign against Hezbollah). In terms of non-Iran news , we didn’t see anything too dramatic since the Thurs close ( China’s inflation was mixed, the Bank of Korea left rates unchanged as expected, TSMC’s Q1 revenue came in a bit better w/Mar sales surging ~45% Y/Y vs. ~22% in Feb, The Information published Space X P&L details from 2025 that seem to fall far short of the types of numbers required for a ~$1.75 T+ market cap, and the latest Barron’s cover story talks about a Space X/Tesla merger ).
• How we’re thinking about markets – there’s still a lot of cynicism and skepticism about the durability of the ceasefire and to the extent the détente’s permanency outperforms expectations (which is our view), this will place further upward pressure on the SPX , especially given the technical backdrop (characterized by lean long positioning and negative sentiment). However, the SPX has already bounced ~8% trough-to-peak in just a few days, and the index is nearing the ~6900-7000 zone , which was a problem area even before the war started . And while we have a brighter view on the Middle East ceasefire than the consensus , the region will never go back to where it stood before (~$90 is probably the new ~$70 when it comes to Brent), and a substantial burst of stagflationary pressure is washing over the global economy from the last 5 weeks of fighting (the US CPI for Mar due out Fri morning is expected to show a significant acceleration vs. Feb).
• What we’ll be watching on Friday – the major events will be the US CPI for Mar at 8:30am ET (the Street is looking for a sharp acceleration in headline to +3.4% vs. +2.4% in Feb while core climbs to +2.7% vs. +2.5% in Feb) and the first meeting of US and Iranian officials in Pakistan (which is set to commence Sat morning Pakistan time). In addition, a hearing will take place before the Court of Int’l Trade about Trump’s 10% Section 122 blanket tariff that was implemented after the SCOTUS decision (and that Trump said would go to 15% but hasn’t).
